steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which excerpt best illustrates that the Kansas farm is “remote and solitary” — meaning isolated from people and other structures.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — in Kansas, "When Dorothy stood in the doorway and looked around, she could see nothing but the great gray prairie on every side. Not a tree nor a house broke the broad sweep of flat country.": concrete visual image of total isolation in Kansas. ✓
  • Choice B — in Kansas, "Once the house had been painted, but the sun blistered the paint and the rains washed it away, and now the house was as dull and gray as everything else.": describes the weathered appearance of the house, not its remoteness from others. ✗
  • Choice C — in Oz, "Dorothy fell asleep only once, and then she dreamed she was in Kansas, where Aunt Em was telling her how glad she was to have her little girl at home again.": set in Oz and focuses on Dorothy’s emotional memory, not Kansas isolation. ✗
  • Choice D — in Oz, "[Dorothy and her companions] walked along listening to the singing of the brightly colored birds and looking at the lovely flowers which now became so thick that the ground was carpeted with them.": set in Oz and describes lush beauty there, not Kansas. ✗
A is correct.
